results.title: Macuse: Give Your AI Superpowers on macOS | Product Hunt
results.content:
Hey Product Hunt,  
  
I built Macuse because AI assistants are getting incredibly good at reasoning, but on macOS they still often hit a wall: they can answer questions, but they cannot reliably act across the apps where your work actually lives.  
  
Macuse is a native macOS app that turns your Mac apps into local tools for AI assistants.  
  
It runs as a local MCP server, connecting Claude, Codex, Cursor, Raycast, and any MCP-compatible client to your Mac. Your AI can manage Calendar events, read and draft Mail, work with Notes and Reminders, search Contacts, send Messages, and use Computer Use to click, type, scroll, and navigate real app interfaces.  
  
A few things I cared about while building it:  
  
• Local-first: your Mac app data is processed locally [...] Subscribe

@konstant\_gk Thank you! Privacy is a core part of Macuse. Macuse runs locally on your Mac and acts as a bridge between your AI client and your Mac apps. It does not store your emails, messages, or reminders on a server. If you use a cloud AI client, the data needed for your request may be sent to that provider, so the final privacy boundary also depends on the AI client/model you choose.

MacAIApps Logo MacAIApps

# Macuse

Give Your AI Superpowers on macOS

Freemium LLM Utilities

Visit Website →

### Overview

Macuse enables AI assistants to directly manage native macOS applications and control any on-screen interface. Through the MCP protocol, it connects Claude Desktop, Cursor, Raycast, and other compatible clients to your Mac's Calendar, Mail, Notes, Contacts, Messages, and more. All processing occurs locally with granular permission controls for privacy.

Pricing: Free (100 daily calls, 1 client) | Lifetime: $29 (unlimited)

Minimum macOS: 13.0 (Ventura)

Architecture: Apple Silicon, Intel

### Key Features [...] Architecture: Apple Silicon, Intel

### Key Features

 Native app integration for Calendar, Mail, Notes, Reminders, Contacts, and Messages
 Universal UI control for buttons, forms, menus, and navigation in any app
 MCP protocol support with one-click setup for Claude Desktop, Cursor, and Raycast
 Privacy-first architecture with all processing done locally on your device
 Granular permission controls requiring explicit approval for all connections
 Works with any MCP-compatible AI client

### Tags

results.title: Macuse MCP Server
results.content:
# Macuse MCP Server

Let your AI assistant directly manage calendar, email, notes, and control any Mac app.

View on macuse.app

## Documentation

## Give Your AI Superpowers on macOS

Let your AI manage your calendar, email, notes, and any Mac app. All on-device.

Download for macOS

## Native App Integrations

Schedule meetings, reply to emails, capture notes, set reminders. Your AI reads and writes to Calendar, Mail, Notes, and more through natural conversation.

Learn more →

## Computer Use

Click, type, and navigate any Mac app. Computer Use for any AI client, working with Claude, Cursor, or any MCP client. Runs in the background without taking over your desktop.

Learn more →

## Works with your tools [...] ### Which AI tools are supported?

Macuse works with Claude Desktop, Cursor, Raycast, and any AI tool that supports the MCP protocol. One installation, all your AI tools connected.

### What can Macuse do?

Manage your calendar, read and reply to emails, take notes, set reminders, search contacts, send messages, and control any Mac application through natural conversation with your AI assistant.

### Do I need technical setup?

No. Macuse auto-detects your AI tools and configures everything with one click. No config files, no terminal commands, no JSON editing.

### What permissions are required? [...] ### What permissions are required?

Macuse requests only the permissions needed for each feature: Calendar, Reminders, Contacts access for those apps, Full Disk Access for Mail and Notes, and Accessibility for Computer Use. You choose what to enable.

### Is Macuse an alternative to OpenAI Codex or Anthropic Computer Use?

Yes. Macuse brings Computer Use to your Mac for any AI client. No ChatGPT Plus or Claude-only lock-in. It runs on-device and works in the background, so your cursor and active window stay untouched while your AI operates other apps.

### What's the difference between Free and Lifetime?

# Macuse — AI Superpowers for Every Mac App

Give your AI direct control of Calendar, Mail, Notes, Reminders, Messages, and any other Mac app over the Model Context Protocol — an MCP server for Mac plus Computer Use for macOS, fully on-device.

Works with Claude Desktop, Cursor, Codex, Raycast, VS Code, Warp, Zed, LM Studio, Windsurf, and any MCP-compatible client.

This repo packages the Claude Desktop `.mcpb` installer for Macuse. The macOS app and full docs live at macuse.app.

`.mcpb`

## What Macuse Does

Two complementary ways for AI to work with your Mac. Everything runs locally — your data never leaves your device.

### Native App Integrations [...] | Toolbox | What you can ask |
 --- |
| Calendar | "Schedule a meeting next Tuesday at 3pm", find free time, manage events |
| Mail | "Draft a reply to this email", search inbox, organize messages |
| Notes | "Find notes mentioning the Q2 launch", capture ideas, append to notes |
| Reminders | Create tasks with due dates and priorities, mark complete, search across lists |
| Messages | Read and send iMessages, search conversations |
| Stickies | Search, read, and create sticky notes in Apple Stickies |
| Contacts | Look up by name, email, phone, or company |
| Maps & Location | Place search, directions, travel time, current location |
| Shortcuts | Browse installed Shortcuts and run them with optional input |

### Computer Use on macOS

results.title: Macuse: AI Automation & Deep Control for macOS Apps
results.content:
© 2026 MCP Market. All rights reserved.·Privacy·Terms

Macuse is a native macOS application designed to give AI assistants like Claude, Cursor, and Raycast advanced control over your computer's interface and native applications through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It transforms natural language commands into real actions, allowing AI to manage your calendar, handle email, work with notes and reminders, navigate web browsers, and interact with complex application interfaces. This tool enables multi-app workflows, turning your AI from a chatbot into a capable digital assistant for a wide range of macOS tasks.

## Key Features
